title: A Spot
content:
In years defaced and lost,
     Two sat here, transport-tossed,
     Lit by a living love
    The wilted world knew nothing of:
     Scared momently
     By gaingivings,
     Then hoping things
     That could not be.

     Of love and us no trace
     Abides upon the place;
     The sun and shadows wheel,
    Season and season sereward steal;
     Foul days and fair
     Here, too, prevail,
     And gust and gale
     As everywhere.

     But lonely shepherd souls
     Who bask amid these knolls
     May catch a faery sound
    On sleepy noontides from the ground:
     "O not again
     Till Earth outwears
     Shall love like theirs
     Suffuse this glen!"
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Hardy, titled "A Spot", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
